violations.detail: Card 1 ('What is the primary function of the mitochondria?') and Card 2 ('Which organelle is responsible for producing most of a cell's ATP?') are mirror-deducible. Knowing one trivially gives you the other. One should be dropped or they should be merged.

Card 3 (cloze testing ATP) is also trivially deducible from Card 1, which already states 'ATP production' as the answer. Three cards all encode essentially the same single fact: mitochondria produce ATP.

violations.rule: Back brevity / parenthetical adds new fact
violations.detail: Back is 'ATP production (cellular energy)' — the parenthetical '(cellular energy)' is a restatement/additional fact, not a standard abbreviation. Per the rules, parenthetical abbreviations like (NHEJ) are OK, but parentheticals that add a NEW fact are not. 'cellular energy' restates what ATP production means, which should be a separate card or removed.

'The mitochondria produces energy in the form of {{c1::ATP}}' — ATP is extremely guessable from context. Anyone who has encountered basic biology would pattern-match 'energy in the form of ___' to ATP. This fails the cloze difficulty test.

All three cards are definitional/label-only. There are zero 'why/how' cards (target ~40%), zero application/reasoning cards (target ~20%), and zero counterintuitive/misconception cards (target ~10%). The set is 100% definitional.

violations.rule: No asking for examples / Label-only cards
violations.detail: Card 2 ('Which organelle is responsible for producing most of a cell's ATP?') is a label-only card — it names the organelle without testing what it does or why. The guide explicitly warns: 'If you can name all the parts but don't know what they do, you understand nothing.'

Even for a simple concept, the minimum is 3-5 meaningful cards. While 3 cards were generated, they all test the same single fact (mitochondria = ATP), so effectively this is a 1-card cluster repeated three times. The topic of mitochondria has rich causal content (why do mitochondria have their own DNA? How does the electron transport chain work? Why is cellular respiration more efficient than glycolysis alone?) that was completely ignored.
All three cards encode the same fact: mitochondria produce ATP. Knowing any one of them gives you the other two for free. This violates the rule against easily derived information.
